category_functions.name: Core_GetTeamsEntryFromIndex
category_functions.description_short: Returns the entry in the Teams table that corresponds to the given teamIndex.
description_extended: NOTE: Use the teams table's .id entry - not the actual index of the table.

category_functions.name: Core_RemovePlayerFromPlayersTable
category_functions.description_short: Removes a player from player and team tables.

category_functions.name: Core_OnGameOver
category_functions.description_short: Call this function to notify other modules (via their ***_OnGameOver() callback) that the match is about to end.
description_extended: Modules typically implement this for cleanup purposes (i.e. remove rules and UI elements) prior to the match end UI.

category_functions.name: EventCue_Create
category_functions.description_short: Creates a clickable event cue that centers camera on target when clicked.
description_extended: Optionally creates a 5-second hintpoint at/on the target and/or invokes a callback function when clicked (only activated once to avoid stacking).

category_functions.name: introtype_Trigger
category_functions.description_short: Spawn the encounter in this instance (i.e. if it was set to trigger manually) or, if it's already spawned and is waiting for it's goal to trigger, triggers that instead.

category_functions.name: Test_SquadNavigationBasics
description_extended: Spawns a specified number of given SBPs at a start-position and orders them to move to the end-position.
The test will count the number of Squads within the group that reached their destination within the
time limit.
The test will finish once all of the specified coordinates are tested
and success will depend upon what % of units reached the end zone (only 100% will succeed)

category_functions.name: Anvil_GetSquadDetachments
category_functions.description_short: Get the squad's detachments

category_functions.name: Anvil_FilterOutForbiddenSquadTypes
category_functions.description_short: Filter out specific types of squads from an SGroup. Add to this function if more types need to be considered as Forbidden
description_extended: remove_airplanes, remove_casualties, remove_auto_builder_squads, remove_building_squads, remove_medic

category_functions.name: Anvil_GetRtsRaceFromMetamapRace
category_functions.description_short: Returns the name of the RTS version of the given race.
description_extended: If a player has a race like "americans_campaign" it will map to "americans." The assumption is that races
are named the same but have an appended "_campaign" to the end

category_functions.name: Anvil_KillNeutralEntitiesNearPosition
category_functions.description_short: Destroy entities, by type or blueprint, inside a marker or sector, or near a position
description_extended: By default this function will kill any entity with the "wreck" unit_type. Use OPT_num_to_kill to specify how many entities
will be destroyed by the function. By default, all matching entities will be destroyed.

category_functions.name: Anvil_SGroupSetTargetingType
category_functions.description_short: Set an sgroup's targeting type (how enemy weapons target the squads)
description_extended: Targeting types are: Targeting_Automatic, Targeting_Manual, Targeting_None

category_functions.name: Anvil_SetPlayerResources
category_functions.description_short: Set a player's resource income. Provide income-per-minute values. For SP missions.
description_extended: Default values are set in anvil.scar. To set all values to default, provide only the player argument.

category_functions.name: Anvil_FindHiddenSpawn
category_functions.description_short: Following a path from origin to destination, finds the HS_CLOSEST/HS_FURTHEST hidden position to/from the destination
description_extended: If no position is found, returns origin. HS_FURTHEST returns origin position if it is hidden.

category_functions.name: Anvil_SetupWreckRecovery
category_functions.description_short: Kill pre-placed vehicle entities and collect their wreck entities into the same egroups.
description_extended: The first argument is a table of egroups like {eg_vehicle1, eg_vehicle2}. The second argument lets you collect all the wrecks into a parent egroup.

category_functions.name: Player_GetAllSquadsNearMarker
category_functions.description_short: Gather together all of a player's squads that are in proximity to a marker, a position, or within a territory sector into an SGroup. The SGroup is cleared beforehand.
description_extended: You can override a marker's normal proximity by specifying a range.

category_functions.name: Player_CanSeeSGroup

category_functions.name: Player_RestrictAddOnList
category_functions.description_short: Restrict a list of addons.
description_extended: list should contain an array of strings to restrict.

category_functions.name: Player_SetMaxCapPopulation
category_functions.description_short: Sets the current personnel or vehicle max-cap for a player. The captype is either CT_Personnel or CT_Vehicle (you can't adjust Medic caps just yet).
description_extended: Note that any modifiers that adjust the current max cap will be applied on top of this.  Also note, this is only adjusting the max cap, not the current cap,
you will have to call Player_SetMaxPopulation to adjust the current max population to do this.

category_functions.name: Player_OwnsSGroup

category_functions.name: Player_OwnsEntity

category_functions.name: Player_SetConstructionMenuAvailability

category_functions.name: Player_GetBuildingID

description_extended: This only looks at completed buildings

category_functions.name: Player_GetBuildingsCount

category_functions.name: Player_OwnsSquad
category_functions.description_short: Returns true if a given player owns a squad

category_functions.name: Player_CanConstructOnPosition
category_functions.description_short: Returns TRUE if player can construct the specified entity at specified position and facing.  Otherwise, returns FALSE.

category_functions.name: Player_GetTotalPopulation
description_extended: Directly uses the C++ API Player_GetCurrentPopulation to get the current population for CT_Personnel,
CT_Vehicle and CT_Medic.

category_functions.name: Player_SetCommandAvailability

category_functions.name: Player_GetAll
category_functions.description_short: Creates/Clears groups that contain all of a player's units and buildings. Defaults - sg_allsquads and eg_allentities
description_extended: Fills an SGroup with all of the given player's squads, and an EGroup with all the player's entities.
If you don't provide and groups, then it defaults to using sg_allsquads and eg_allentities.

category_functions.name: Player_GetSquadConcentration
category_functions.description_short: Finds the greatest (or least) concentration of squads owned by a player.
description_extended: This function is slow, so don't call it very often

category_functions.name: Player_RestrictBuildingList

category_functions.name: Player_GetEntityConcentration

description_extended: This function is slow, so don't call it very often

category_functions.name: Player_HasBuildingUnderConstruction

category_functions.name: Player_HasBuilding

description_extended: This only looks at completed buildings - use Player_HasBuildingUnderConstruction to see if the player is building something

category_functions.name: Player_GetEntitiesFromType
category_functions.description_short: Returns an EGroup containing all of the players entities of a specific unit_type (as defined by the type_ext on the entity)

category_functions.name: Player_AddResources

description_extended: Note that any modifiers that adjust the current cap will be applied on top of this.  Also note, the current cap cannot go higher than the max cap.

category_functions.name: Player_SetSquadProductionAvailability

category_functions.name: Player_HasBuildingsExcept

category_functions.name: Player_GetBuildingsCountOnly

category_functions.name: Player_AddResource
category_functions.description_short: Add resource to player, as opposed to just setting it.

category_functions.name: Player_CanSeeEGroup
category_functions.description_short: Returns true if a player can see ALL or ANY items in an egroup

category_functions.name: Player_ClearArea
category_functions.description_short: Any of the player's units in the marker area move out of the area, and can be made invulnerable for a bit whilst they do it
description_extended: You can replace the marker with a position and a range - i.e. Player_ClearArea(player, pos, range, invulnerable)

category_functions.name: Player_OwnsEGroup

category_functions.name: Player_IsAllied

category_functions.name: Player_GetPopulationPercentage
category_functions.description_short: Gets the current personnel or vehicle population as a percetange of the current max-cap. The captype is either CT_Personnel or CT_Vehicle.
description_extended: If MaxPopulation is 0, returns 1.0
captype is CT_Personnel by default.

category_functions.name: Player_SetAbilityAvailability
category_functions.description_short: Sets the availability of an ability. Availability can be either ITEM_LOCKED, ITEM_UNLOCKED, ITEM_REMOVED or ITEM_DEFAULT

category_functions.name: Player_AddSquadsToSGroup

category_functions.name: Player_GetBuildingsCountExcept

category_functions.name: PrefabHelper_StandardTriggerSystem
category_functions.description_short: Add this helper function to your prefab's Init function to cover standard trigger zone functionality.
description_extended: It will call a Trigger function in your prefab when appropriate. Your schema needs to include the standard elements: trigger_enable, ignore_planes, trigger_player, trigger_zone and delay

category_functions.name: PrintOnScreen_Add
category_functions.description_short: Prints the lua content of an object on the screen
description_extended: Prints the given message on the screen. If you would like to print multiple messages without clearing them you can
assign them unique ID's. Must use PrintOnScreen_Remove(id) to remove each instance

category_functions.name: PrintOnScreen_RemoveFromScreen
category_functions.description_short: Remove any messages from the screen
description_extended: Removes from the screen any messages put there with PrintOnScreen()

category_functions.name: PrintOnScreen_Remove
category_functions.description_short: Removes the PrintOnScreen text of a given ID

category_functions.name: Event_IsAnyRunning
category_functions.description_short: Returns true if any event is currently running [at or below (more important than) the priority threshold. If not specified, ignores threshold.]

category_functions.name: Prefab_IsActive
category_functions.description_short: Checks if a prefab instance is currently running
description_extended: If the prefab has a prefabName_IsActive() function, that will be called. Otherwise, an error will be thrown

category_functions.name: Prefab_GetAllOfType
category_functions.description_short: Returns a table of all current prefabs matching the instance type

category_functions.name: Prefab_GetNameSetOfType
category_functions.description_short: Returns a table of all current prefabs matching the instance type where the key to each entry in the table is the name of the prefab

category_functions.name: Prefab_ForcePostInit
category_functions.description_short: Finalize the initialization of any prefab instances that have been run through Prefab_Init(). This is automatically called one frame after a prefab is initialized, but you can force it early if necessary.
description_extended: If you are creating prefabs and using them in the same frame, call this function once, after all the Prefab_Inits, but before you start using them. This will force the post-initialization to occur early.

category_functions.name: Prefab_Init
category_functions.description_short: Initialize a specific prefab instance. In many cases, this is all you need to do (see the documentation for the specific prefab for details)
description_extended: restore_table can be passed optionally to provoke the prefab type specific RestoreFromTable to be called before the type specific init function.

category_functions.name: Prefab_Trigger
category_functions.description_short: Triggers the specified prefab

category_functions.name: Prefab_ApplyDefaults
category_functions.description_short: Applies the defaults listed in the prefab schema to the instance data. Any items NOT included explicitly in the instance data will have default values pulled from the schema.

category_functions.name: Prefab_Reset
category_functions.description_short: Stop and restart a prefab instance, pulling a fresh set of the instance data from the WB-exported prefabs. This is meant for testing and debug purposes, rather than use in mission scripts.

category_functions.name: Prefab_Stop
category_functions.description_short: Stops a prefab instance from continuing to run.
description_extended: If the prefab has a prefabName_Stop() function, that will be called. Also, all events with the instance as the data parameter will be removed.

category_functions.name: Prefab_PresetComplete
category_functions.description_short: Called after Preset is called on all modules

category_functions.name: Prefab_GetInstance
category_functions.description_short: Returns the instance data for a given instance, specified by a number of methods.
description_extended: This normalizes your instance data. You can pass in the instance name as a string, a data table with an instance subtable (i.e. the input params from an event function) or a real instance data table, and it normalizes the format and returns you the instance data table.

category_functions.name: Prefab_DoAction
category_functions.description_short: Calls an action function with a given name on an instance, making sure it calls the right version for the type of Prefab. Instance can be a string, this will convert it.
description_extended: Example: Calling the "Trigger" action on an instance that is a PlaneCrash prefab will look for a function called "PlaneCrash_Trigger"

category_functions.name: Prefab_IsValid
category_functions.description_short: Returns if a String is a valid prefab

category_functions.name: AIMoveGoal_SetOverrideGoalData

category_functions.name: AIMoveGoal_SetModifyGoalData

category_functions.description_short: Returns a fixed size table of markers from the world builder.  Markers that do not

description_extended: mkr_table = Marker_GetTable( 'mkr_%d' ) -- creates a table with 3 markers named 'mkr_1', 'mkr_2', and 'mkr_3' (and so on) from the WB\n\n

category_functions.name: Prox_SquadsInProximityOfSquads
category_functions.description_short: Checks if ALL or ANY squads are in proximity of a given squad group.
description_extended: Set "all" to true to check that ALL squads are in proximity, or set "all" to false to check for ANY.

category_functions.name: Prox_MarkerSGroup
category_functions.description_short: Returns the distance between a marker and a squad group.  use checktype PROX_SHORTEST, PROX_LONGEST, or PROX_CENTER.
description_extended: If check is PROX_SHORTEST this will return the shortest distance between the two groups.\n
If check is PROX_LONGEST this will return the longest distance between the two groups.\n
If check is PROX_CENTER this will return the distance between the two groups centers.\n

category_functions.name: Prox_PlayerEntitiesInProximityOfPlayerSquads
category_functions.description_short: Checks if ALL or ANY players entities are in proximity of a given squad group.
description_extended: Set "all" to true to check that ALL entities are in proximity, or set "all" to false to check for ANY.

category_functions.name: Prox_AreSquadsNearMarker
category_functions.description_short: Returns true if ANY or ALL squads from a group are in range of a given position, marker, or territory sector
description_extended: You MUST specify a range if you are using a position rather than a marker.\n
Markers with proximity type rectangle will use circular proximity check if custom range is supplied\n

category_functions.name: Prox_PlayerSquadsInProximityOfSquads
category_functions.description_short: Checks if ALL or ANY players squads are in proximity of a given squad group.
description_extended: Set "all" to true to check that ALL squads are in proximity, or set "all" to false to check for ANY.

category_functions.name: Prox_AreTeamsNearMarker
category_functions.description_short: Returns true if ANY or ALL of a teams's squads are in range of a given position, marker, or territory sector. THIS FUNCTION IS VERY SLOW. DO NOT USE THIS UNLESS ABSOLUTELY NECESSARY.
description_extended: You MUST specify a range if you are using a position rather than a marker.\n
Markers with proximity type rectangle will use circular proximity check if custom range is supplied\n

category_functions.name: NIS_StoreCurrentCameraPos
category_functions.description_short: Stores the current camera position as the NIS's end_camera position.
description_extended: Useful if you want the camera to always end where the player was last looking. If it does not return a position, nothing will be stored.

category_functions.name: NIS_END
category_functions.description_short: ONLY USE THIS INSIDE AN NIS EVENT. Transition from the NIS back to the game.
description_extended: You can safely do any cleanup after calling this, and it will occur once the screen is black.

category_functions.name: NIS_StartDrift
category_functions.description_short: ONLY USE THIS INSIDE AN NIS EVENT with an NIS camera. Starts the NIS camera drifting.
description_extended: "Drifting" moves the camera around in the shape of an infinity symbol, so it doesn't look quite so static.

category_functions.name: NIS_START
category_functions.description_short: ONLY USE THIS INSIDE AN NIS EVENT. Transition from the game (or a preamble) into an NIS.
description_extended: You can do any setup after calling this, as that will occur once the screen has faded to black on the transition in. The game will fade back in to the NIS over the next half second or so (apart from opening cinematics, which is two seconds), so you may want to delay your first action accordingly. The details table should include, at the very least, the following items: style (NIS_OPENING, NIS_MID, or NIS_CLOSING), revealFOW (a Boolean). If you included details in a preamble, you don't need to supply them again here.

category_functions.name: NIS_FadeCameraCut
category_functions.description_short: Used to create a fade cut in an NIS
description_extended: Call this in your NIS Event.  This will require an nis_pause() frame in your NIS file in order to work.

category_functions.name: NIS_PREAMBLE
category_functions.description_short: ONLY USE THIS INSIDE AN NIS EVENT. Transition from the game into an NIS preamble - a quick section before the NIS proper. A preamble is totally optional, if you want to go straight into an NIS, skip this and use NIS_START instead.
description_extended: See NIS_START for information about the details table.

category_functions.name: NIS_StopDrift
category_functions.description_short: ONLY USE THIS INSIDE AN NIS EVENT with an NIS camera. Stops the NIS camera drifting.
description_extended: "Drifting" moves the camera around in the shape of an infinity symbol, so it doesn't look quite so static.

category_functions.name: NIS_CameraCheckpoint
category_functions.description_short: ONLY USE THIS INSIDE AN NIS EVENT. Wait until the NIS camera hits its counterpart checkpoint.
description_extended: Makes the script pause and wait until the camera portion hits "nis_pause()" (the counterpart checkpoint) on it's timeline. With these two functions, you can have the event script and camera sync up - i.e. make one wait for the other - at various points in the NIS and avoid all sorts of timing issues.

category_functions.name: Camera_PanOverTimeTo
category_functions.description_short: Queue moving the camera to an entity/marker/pos/egroup/sgroup/squad within seconds.
description_extended: The camera will move from the position that was in front of it in the queue.

The first parameter of the supplied function must be SquadID

category_functions.name: SGroup_Filter

description_extended: A table of blueprints can be specified if you want to filter on more than one type.
Setting filtertype to FILTER_KEEP results in the group only containing squads of the types listed in the blueprint table.
Setting filtertype to FILTER_REMOVE will strip those same squads out and leave those that aren't of the types listed.
Setting splitSGroup will move any squads being KEEP or REMOVE to it.  This SGroup will NOT be cleared beforehand.
SquadTypes can also be used in place of Blueprints. If you want a squad to match a bunch of SquadTypes at the same time (i.e. AND instead of OR) then they can be listed together in a sub-table.
